_comment: Data reliability scores per country per indicator. Range 0.0-1.0. Reviewed quarterly. See edict-004.

CN.note: Official GDP widely questioned; credit data from BIS more reliable than domestic sources

XM.note: Eurostat methodology, generally reliable; aggregation masks country-level variance

KR.note: Bank of Korea data generally reliable

IN.note: GDP methodology revised 2015, base year debate; employment data sparse

BR.note: IBGE generally reliable but political pressure periods exist

TR.note: CPI widely disputed: official 85% vs independent estimates >170% (2022). Credit data from BIS more reliable

AR.note: CPI data faked 2007-2015 under Kirchner. Post-Milei improving but legacy trust issues

ZA.note: Stats SA reasonably reliable; employment survey methodology limitations

SA.note: Oil GDP transparent but non-oil GDP opaque; limited credit market data

AE.note: Similar to SA; Dubai more transparent than other emirates
